common.name: Name
common.readOnlyTooltip: Shared with you as read-only; editing and deletion are disabled.

error.description: The page hit an unexpected error. You can retry or go back home.

platform.title: Enterprise Agent Platform
platform.subtitle: Manage agents, models/runtimes, knowledge, tools, workflows, approvals, audit, and run records from one enterprise control console.

empty.noDataDescription: There are no records to show yet.
empty.filteredDescription: Adjust the filters or clear them to view more records.
empty.errorDescription: The data could not be loaded. Try again.
empty.noAccessDescription: The current identity does not have permission to view this data.

stats.agentsHelper: Business agents for conversation and orchestration

appCenter.title: Enterprise Agent App Center
appCenter.description: Publish agent apps from business templates, bind models, knowledge, tools, and access policy, then move into the run, approval, and audit loop.
appCenter.selectToInspectHelper: Click a template or published app to inspect resources, readiness, and the next action.
appCenter.noIssues: No blocking issues. This can move to the next step.
appCenter.templateRuntime: Memory and workflow can be enabled during publish

scenarios.title: Business scenario loop
scenarios.description: Package workflows, tool permissions, approvals, and audit into directly runnable enterprise scenarios.

opsTasks.title: Open operations tasks
opsTasks.description: Bring approvals, failed workflows, unready agents, and tool policy risks into one action queue.

governanceHealth.title: Enterprise governance snapshot
governanceHealth.description: Put tenants, identities, approvals, and audit on the platform home screen so you can confirm the agent has the basic governance needed for enterprise workflows.
governanceHealth.tenantsHelper: Separates data and policies across departments, business lines, or customers.
governanceHealth.identitiesHelper: Users, roles, and service identities currently visible to permission policy.
governanceHealth.pendingApprovalsHelper: High-risk actions waiting for human approval before execution.
governanceHealth.auditEventsHelper: Tool, agent, and workflow runs are written to audit records.
governanceHealth.auditEventsFailedHelper: {{count}} audit event(s) failed. Check backend logs or storage configuration.

dashboard.title: Enterprise agent operations
dashboard.description: Bring approvals, workflow runs, risky actions, and audit events into the first screen. This answers what the platform can do now, where a human must step in, and where to click next.
dashboard.pendingApprovalsDescription: {{approved}} approved. Risky actions must carry an approval ID before execution.
dashboard.recentRunsDescription: Check whether recent automation workflows completed, partially completed, or failed.
dashboard.riskActionsDescription: Risky tools and workflows trigger approval so the governance loop can be tested.
dashboard.auditTrailDescription: Enterprise tool calls are written to audit records with user, tenant, and input context.
dashboard.emptyAudit: No audit events yet. Run an agent or tool first.
dashboard.workflowHealthDescription: See which automations are enabled, completed, partially completed, or failing.
dashboard.recommendedActionsDescription: Next steps based on model, agent, approval, and workflow readiness.
dashboard.todoReady: Model, agent, approval, and audit paths are ready. Continue by connecting real business systems.

operations.title: Business agent fleet
operations.headlineEmpty: No active business agents yet. Publish an agent from a business template, then connect department tools, approvals, and workflows.
operations.headlineNeedsWork: {{count}} agent(s) still need configuration. Complete models, knowledge, or tools before production runs.
operations.headlineApprovals: Agents are runnable, but {{count}} risky action(s) still need human approval.
operations.headlineReady: All active agents are ready. Continue by running business questions or connecting real enterprise systems.
operations.emptyApprovals: No approvals need review right now.
operations.readyIssue: Model, knowledge, tools, and runtime are ready. Run a business question directly.
operations.missingIssue: Required configuration is missing. Open agent management to complete the model, knowledge, or tools.
operations.archivedIssue: This agent is archived and will not appear in the runner.

console.title: Platform console
console.description: The AgentFoundry console combines models and runtimes, knowledge, tools, workflows, and audit into a governed, runnable enterprise agent platform.
console.agentsDescription: Publish HR, support, ops, analytics, and other business agents.
console.resourcesDescription: Attach models, knowledge bases, tools, long-term memory, and enterprise data sources.
console.runDescription: Ask a real business question and let the agent use its configured knowledge and tools.
console.governanceDescription: Review tool permissions, traces, audit logs, and workflow history.

now.title: Current runnable enterprise agent
now.description: Run the default instance first. It calls enterprise tools as the current tenant user, returns an answer, and writes tool traces to audit.
now.noAgentDescription: You can quick-publish the default business agent. The platform will attach the current model, existing knowledge bases, and template tools.

model.title: Confirm the model first
model.description: An enterprise agent needs a model credential before it can answer questions and decide whether to call tools.

publish.title: Do one thing next: publish the first business agent
publish.description: Use the first business template, attach the current model, existing knowledge bases, and template tools, then go straight to a test run.

configure.title: Make the published agent runnable first
configure.description: Bind a model and review knowledge, tools, and approval risks before business testing.

run.title: Run a real business question now
run.description: The agent is published. Use the sample question to verify model, knowledge, tool calls, and audit in one pass.

governance.title: It has run once. Review traces and audit next
governance.description: Check whether tools executed under tenant permissions, whether knowledge hits make sense, and whether audit records were written.

workbench.title: Start here today
workbench.description: Bring runnable agents, approvals, workflows, and long-term memory into the first screen. Check status, then jump directly into runs, governance, or automation.
workbench.readinessDescription: Check model, knowledge, enterprise connectors, members, agents, and workflows in launch order.
workbench.riskEmpty: No blocking items right now. Continue with an agent run or workflow run.

members.title: Members and roles
members.description: {{count}} active member(s) can join enterprise sessions.

firstAgentGuide.title: First enterprise agent launch guide
firstAgentGuide.description: Move through the first production-readiness loop from real state: model, publish, run, and governance. The primary button jumps to the current next step.

model.title: Prepare model
model.ready: {{count}} model credential(s) are available for enterprise agents.
model.empty: No usable model yet. Configure model credentials first.

agent.title: Publish agent
agent.partial: {{count}} agent(s) are published. Continue checking runtime readiness.
agent.empty: Quickly publish an enterprise agent from a business template.

run.title: Validate run
run.ready: A run result is available for answer, knowledge hit, and tool-call review.
run.partial: A runnable agent is selected. Send a business question next.
run.empty: After publishing an agent, run one real business question.

governance.title: Close governance loop
governance.ready: {{count}} audit event(s) are available for governance tracing.
governance.pending: {{count}} approval(s) are waiting, useful for permission-block validation.
governance.empty: After a run, review approvals, audit, and access records.

rolloutPath.title: Enterprise agent platform rollout path
rolloutPath.description: The knowledge base can stay as-is for now. Use these six steps to connect model, agent, runtime, governance, and portable configuration.

model.title: Configure model
model.description: Confirm at least one model credential is available for agent reasoning and tool routing.

knowledge.title: Connect knowledge
knowledge.description: Bind enterprise documents, policies, or business material; the current knowledge base is enough to continue.

agent.title: Publish agent
agent.description: Publish an enterprise agent instance from a business template with model, knowledge, tools, and permissions.

run.title: Validate runtime
run.description: Run one real business question to check answers, knowledge hits, tool calls, and audit.

governance.title: Approve and audit
governance.description: Review risky actions, permission blocks, tool records, and run traces.

config.title: Import/export
config.description: Export members, agents, workflows, and policies for configuration migration and team reproduction.

risks.errors: API or loading errors are present. Check governance and runtime status first.
risks.approvals: {{count}} high-risk action(s) are waiting for approval.

indicators.readyAgentsHelper: Published business agents that passed readiness checks.
indicators.approvalsHelper: Risky tools or workflows waiting for human review.

run.title: Run a real business question
run.descriptionReady: Current run uses {{agent}} to verify model, knowledge, tools, and audit.
run.descriptionEmpty: No runnable agent yet. Publish a business agent first.

workflow.title: Start automation workflow
workflow.description: {{count}} workflow template(s) are available for analytics, support, or approval loops.

governance.title: Review approvals and audit
governance.description: {{count}} pending approval(s). Tool calls are written to audit.

memory.title: Review long-term memory
memory.description: Memory read/write evidence from {{count}} agent run(s) is aggregated here.

launchpad.title: Enterprise agent platform rollout
launchpad.description: Complete the launch path in order: members and tenants, model, knowledge base, agent publishing, trial run, and governance audit. The knowledge base can stay as-is for now and expand later.

members.title: 1. Set members and tenants
members.description: Add departments, roles, users, and status first so published agents can enforce tenant isolation and access control.

model.title: 2. Configure model
model.description: The platform needs at least one model credential to drive chat, tool routing, and workflow reasoning.

knowledge.title: 3. Bind knowledge base
knowledge.description: Prepare enterprise material, policies, tickets, or business documents so published agents can bind them for the right tenant.

agent.title: 4. Publish business agent
agent.description: Generate a runnable enterprise agent instance from a template and bind model, knowledge, tools, memory, and access scope.

run.title: 5. Trial run agent
run.description: Run a published agent with a real question to verify the model, knowledge, tool calls, and access policy work together.

governance.title: 6. Governance and audit
governance.description: Review approvals, permission blocks, tool execution, and agent run records to close the traceability loop before launch.

orchestration.title: Assemble an enterprise agent into a releasable app
orchestration.description: Follow this path to complete the template, model, knowledge base, tools, tenant policy, publishing, and test run. This is the main operating desk for the enterprise agent platform.

template.title: Choose business template
template.description: Decide which business scenario this agent serves, such as knowledge retrieval, ticket handling, or data analysis.

model.title: Bind model
model.description: Select an enterprise model credential for reasoning, tool routing, and summary generation.

knowledge.title: Bind knowledge base
knowledge.description: Attach policies, documents, tickets, and business material so answers have enterprise context.

tools.title: Authorize tools
tools.description: Give the agent access to database queries, external APIs, code execution, and other tools with scoped permissions.

policy.title: Set tenant and runtime policy
policy.description: Configure users, roles, long-term memory, workflow switches, and runtime boundaries.

publish.title: Publish instance
publish.description: Publish the configured agent as a tenant-scoped instance callable by enterprise users.

operate.title: Test run and govern
operate.description: Run a real question, then inspect workflow, approvals, tool traces, and audit records.

monitoring.title: Runtime monitoring
monitoring.description: Monitor agent and workflow runs in one operating surface, including status, duration, runtime, and governance events.
monitoring.pendingApprovalsHelper: High-risk actions waiting for human confirmation.
monitoring.recentAgentRunsHelper: Open one to inspect the full test-run result.
monitoring.recentWorkflowRunsHelper: Check whether automation completed, partially completed, or failed.
monitoring.recentAuditHelper: Shows recent platform audit events. Use Audit for full search.
monitoring.runQueueDescription: Aggregates recent run status, objects, and timestamps for failure triage and governance follow-up.
monitoring.focusFailuresHelper: Failed records are marked with a red rail. Open one to inspect object, time, and step status.
monitoring.focusRuntimeHelper: Runtime issues are usually diagnosed with provider health, approvals, and audit records.
monitoring.runDetailDescription: Inspect the selected run object, status, time, steps, and governance action.
monitoring.noRunsDescription: There are no agent or workflow run records yet. Run an agent or workflow first.
monitoring.auditSearchDescription: Trace platform operations by tenant, user, tool, and result.
monitoring.keywordPlaceholder: Search agent, workflow, input, or output

tenantGovernance.title: Tenant Governance
tenantGovernance.description: Pick an enterprise identity first, then run agents, tools, and workflows as that identity.

tenantWorkspace.title: Multi-tenant run view
tenantWorkspace.description: Bring enterprise identity, department data, tool permissions, and audit entry points together before the agent takes business action.
tenantWorkspace.emptyTenants: No tenant data yet. Configure or load enterprise identities and department resources in the connector center first.
tenantWorkspace.noIdentity: No enterprise identities yet. Configure or load identity data in the connector center first.

memoryOps.title: Memory operations
memoryOps.description: Group loaded run history by tenant, user, and agent to show memory hits and writes, so you can verify whether long-term memory is actually participating in business answers.
memoryOps.empty: No memory operations data yet. Run an agent with long-term memory enabled, or load agent run history first.
memoryOps.scopeDescription: Review long-term memory hits, writes, and source coverage by tenant, user, and agent.
memoryOps.searchPlaceholder: Search agent, tenant, user, or source
memoryOps.emptyFilteredDescription: Clear filters or broaden the criteria to view memory scopes.
memoryOps.selectedScopeDescription: Review recent Q&A, hits, writes, and source evidence.
memoryOps.selectScopeDescription: Select a memory scope to inspect its evidence.
memoryOps.sourceCoverageDescription: Use this to see whether long-term memory relies on too few sources.
memoryOps.noSourcesDescription: This memory scope does not have source evidence yet.
memoryOps.statLoadedRunsHelper: Run history is connected to long-term memory statistics
memoryOps.statMemoryWritesHelper: Records saved into memory after runs

accessControl.title: Enterprise access center
accessControl.description: Review tool grants, deny reasons, and pending approvals by tenant, role, and identity before an agent acts for the business.
accessControl.noPendingQueue: No pending approvals for this identity.
accessControl.noIdentity: No enterprise identities yet. Connect identity data, then configure roles and tool access.

workflowOps.title: Enterprise workflow operations
workflowOps.description: Bring workflow templates, the current execution agent, human approvals, and recent runs into one entry point for analytics, ticket follow-up, policy review, and other automated business flows.
workflowOps.fallbackDescription: This workflow comes from the default configuration. Open the runner to adjust inputs and execute it.
workflowOps.noSteps: No steps defined yet. Open the workflow runner to inspect or enable templates.
workflowOps.noRuns: No runs yet. Run the current workflow to create the first record.

triggerOps.title: Trigger center
triggerOps.description: Connect schedules, manual workflow runs, approval gates, and audit entry points so enterprise agents can execute on a business cadence instead of only answering questions.
triggerOps.summaryManual: Workflows are still mainly triggered manually. Stabilize inputs first, then schedule the key flows.
triggerOps.summaryPaused: Triggers exist, but all are disabled. Enable one to turn the flow into automated operations.
triggerOps.summaryActive: {{count}} enabled triggers can drive agents to run automatically.
triggerOps.manualTrigger: Run the current workflow manually for trials and recovery runs.
triggerOps.approvalGate: Route higher-risk workflows through approval before writing audit records.
triggerOps.loadFailed: Failed to load schedules. Open the schedule page and retry.
triggerOps.noSchedules: No schedules yet. Create one to turn a stable workflow into automated execution.

connectors.title: Data source connectors
connectors.description: See whether enterprise tools read local connector data or a real business API, and check connector readiness.
connectors.runtimeDescription: The connector configuration used by tools, agents, and workflows for the current tenant.
connectors.testDescription: Enter your enterprise gateway URL. The platform will test policy, ticket, and department metrics APIs.
connectors.draftDescription: After saving, this becomes the runtime connector config for tenant {{tenant}}. Agents, tools, and workflows reuse it.
connectors.savedConfigsDescription: Save enterprise API URLs, paths, and token status per tenant so agents can reuse them at runtime.
connectors.savedConfigsEmpty: No saved connector configs yet. Fill the form below and save a config.
connectors.saveSuccess: Connector config saved. The token will not be shown again.
connectors.saveSuccessWithTenant: Connector config for tenant {{tenant}} saved. The token will not be shown again.
connectors.saveError: Failed to save enterprise API connector config.
connectors.saveBaseUrlRequired: Enter the enterprise API URL first.
connectors.applyEnvHint: After the test passes, save it as a tenant connector config. Tokens are stored only as status and are not shown again.
connectors.testBaseUrlRequired: Enter the enterprise API URL first.
connectors.testBeforeSaveRequired: The test did not fully pass, so it was not saved as a runtime config.
connectors.validationBaseUrlProtocol: The enterprise API URL must start with http:// or https://.
connectors.loadError: Unable to read data source connector status.

members.title: Members and roles
members.description: Maintain enterprise members, tenants, roles, and active status. Agent runtime permissions use these roles.
members.permissionNote: Agent permissions use roles and members

agents.title: Published agents
agents.description: Agents the current user can run or govern in the enterprise platform.
agents.empty: No agent instances yet. Publish one from a business template, then bind models, tools, knowledge, and governance policies.

agentManagement.title: Agent management
agentManagement.description: Publish tenant-scoped agent instances from business templates. Permissions, tools, memory, and audit can then attach to the instance.
agentManagement.configurationDescription: Bind a business template to a model, knowledge bases, tools, and runtime policies before publishing it.

lifecycle.title: Agent lifecycle workspace
lifecycle.description: Select a published agent first, then handle run, workflow, editing, and governance actions.
lifecycle.emptyDescription: No runnable agent has been published yet. Configure and publish one from the templates below.
lifecycle.selectDescription: The list shows published agents in the enterprise. Select one to inspect capabilities, tools, knowledge bases, access scope, and governance actions.
lifecycle.noKnowledgeOrCapabilities: No knowledge bases or capability tags bound

opsCenter.title: Agent operations center
opsCenter.description: Check instances, models, knowledge bases, tools, and permissions before runtime debugging and audit review.

agentManagement.configurationCollapsedHint: Expand templates, rollout pipeline, and publish checks

runnerSection.title: Runtime debugging
runnerSection.description: Select a published agent to send an enterprise request and inspect routing, tool calls, knowledge sources, and audit results.

agentManagement.readinessNoIssues: Readiness checks are satisfied. You can run a test.
agentManagement.repairHint: Handle these items in edit configuration or approvals.
agentManagement.selectTemplateHint: Choose a template on the left, then configure the enterprise resources it should use.
agentManagement.name: Agent name
agentManagement.accessScopeDescription: Leave users and roles empty to allow every enterprise identity. Once selected, only matching users or roles can run this agent.
agentManagement.accessTenantHint: Only members and roles in tenant {{tenant}} are shown.
agentManagement.unavailableResource: Resource is not in the current available list
agentManagement.noModel: No model credential yet. Add one in model configuration first.
agentManagement.noKnowledge: No knowledge base yet. This instance will be published without attached knowledge for now.
agentManagement.releaseSummaryDescription: Review tenant, model, knowledge, tools, access scope, and runtime policy before publishing.
agentManagement.releaseMissingModel: Bind a model credential before publishing.
agentManagement.releaseNoKnowledge: No knowledge base is attached. The published agent will answer with the model and tools only.

ops.noRuntimeAgentHint: Publish an instance, then select it from the published list.

pipeline.title: Agent release pipeline
pipeline.description: Track template, model, knowledge, tools, runtime policy, publishing, and governance as one release path.
pipeline.governanceDetail: Route runs into approvals and audit

agentManagement.emptyAgents: No published instances yet. Publish one from the left.
agentManagement.loadError: Unable to read agent management data.

wizard.title: Setup guide
wizard.knowledgeMissing: Select at least one knowledge base
wizard.toolsMissing: Select at least one template tool
wizard.ready: Core configuration is ready. You can publish or save it.

quickActions.title: Next actions
quickActions.description: Open each module from here to complete the platform setup.

runtime.title: Platform runtime
runtime.description: Read the current user, tenant, connector, audit, and storage paths from the platform API.
runtime.error: Unable to read platform runtime status. Make sure the platform API is running.

policy.title: Tool authorization policy
policy.description: Enterprise tools are not available to every caller. This shows tool authorization for the current user and tenant.
policy.empty: No tool authorization results yet. Start the platform API to see whether each tool may be called.
policy.error: Unable to read the tool authorization policy.

subagents.title: Built-in agent templates
subagents.description: The platform can create collaboration agents for business roles, such as policy researchers and workflow operators.
subagents.error: Unable to read built-in agent templates.

agentRunner.title: Agent business Q&A
agentRunner.description: Enter one business question. The platform uses model routing first, falls back to rules when the model is unavailable, selects an enterprise tool, and returns an answer.
agentRunner.noInstances: No runnable instance yet. Publish one in Agent management first.
agentRunner.question: Business question
agentRunner.placeholder: For example: check the status of INC-1001
agentRunner.approvalIdPlaceholder: Required for approved high-risk tool runs
agentRunner.readyAfterPublishTitle: Recently published agent is ready to run
agentRunner.readyAfterPublishDescription: {{name}} is selected automatically. The current question will run with tenant, identity, knowledge, tools, and audit settings.
agentRunner.accessDenied: Current identity cannot run this agent
agentRunner.conversationEmpty: This agent has no run history yet. Ask a question to keep the Q&A and run evidence here.
agentRunner.historyLoadError: Unable to load agent run history.
agentRunner.historyClearError: Unable to clear agent run history.
agentRunner.approvalRequiredCreated: This agent called a high-risk tool. An approval request was created; approve it in Governance before running again.
agentRunner.emptyResult: Run a business question to see the agent answer and tool trace here.
agentRunner.fallbackNotice: Model routing did not complete, so this run used the rules fallback. Reason:
agentRunner.memoryHitsEmpty: This run did not match this agent's long-term memory. Run once, then ask a related follow-up to see hits.
agentRunner.knowledgeHitsEmpty: This run did not match the knowledge bases attached to this agent.
agentRunner.knowledgeError: Knowledge search did not fully complete.

toolRunner.title: Enterprise tool runner
toolRunner.description: Call an enterprise tool directly to verify authorization, connector access, and audit logging.
toolRunner.approvalIdPlaceholder: Required for approved high-risk tool runs
toolRunner.approvalRequiredCreated: This tool requires approval. An approval request was created; approve it in Governance before running again.
toolRunner.noResultYet: This run did not return a structured result.
toolRunner.emptyResult: Run a tool to see its response here.
toolRunner.notAllowed: The current user is not allowed to call this tool
toolRunner.notConfiguredForAgent: The selected agent has not enabled this tool

workflowRunner.title: Automated workflow runner
workflowRunner.description: Choose an enterprise workflow, let the agent call tools step by step, and record every step in audit logs.
workflowRunner.approvalIdPlaceholder: Required for approved high-risk workflow runs
workflowRunner.approvalRequiredCreated: This workflow requires approval. An approval request was created; approve it in Governance before running again.
workflowRunner.emptyResult: Run a workflow to see the summary, execution steps, and tool call records here.
workflowRunner.templatesDescription: Enable platform workflows and review which tools each template will call.
workflowRunner.historyDescription: Review recent automation inputs, status, and result summaries.
workflowRunner.historyLoadError: Unable to load workflow run history.
workflowRunner.confirmDisableBody: Disabled templates cannot be selected for new workflow runs or approval requests until they are enabled again.
workflowRunner.disableImpactScope: {{steps}} steps, {{tools}} tools will be removed from the active workflow list.

approvals.title: Approval center
approvals.description: Register high-risk tools, workflows, and agent actions as approval requests before an owner approves or rejects them.
approvals.createDescription: Simulate the human approval entry point in an enterprise platform. This can later gate real tools and workflows.
approvals.agentToolApprovalReason: Request run approval for {{agent}} to call {{tool}}
approvals.listDescription: Review pending and completed governance requests.
approvals.approveAndRunError: Approval was submitted, but automatic continuation failed.
approvals.emptyFilteredDescription: Clear filters or broaden the criteria to review more approval requests.
approvals.confirmApproveBody: This marks the request as approved. The approval ID can then be used to run the protected tool, workflow, or agent action.
approvals.confirmApproveAndRunBody: This approves the request and immediately continues the associated tool or workflow with the recorded inputs.
approvals.confirmRejectBody: This rejects the request and blocks the protected operation from running with this approval ID.

toolCatalog.title: Tool center
toolCatalog.description: Review each enterprise tool's authorization, bound agents, and call statistics.
toolCatalog.loadError: Unable to read tool center data.
toolCatalog.searchPlaceholder: Search tool name, description, or input key
toolCatalog.emptyDescription: After tool providers or tool policies are connected, governable enterprise tools will appear here.
toolCatalog.emptyFilteredDescription: Clear filters or adjust the keyword to review more tools.

audit.title: Audit events
audit.description: Search platform operations, tool calls, and run-related events by actor, resource, action, result, and time.
audit.loadError: Unable to read filtered audit events.
audit.eventDetailDescription: Review event context, call inputs, result payload, and errors.
audit.eventErrorFallback: The call failed. Details were written to the audit event.
audit.empty: No tool calls yet. Run an agent and trigger a tool to see audit events here.
audit.error: Unable to read tool audit events.

configManagement.title: Configuration management
configManagement.description: Export and import portable platform configuration for configuration migration, backups, and team reproduction.
configManagement.redactedNotice: Connector tokens are not exported; importing redacted config will not overwrite existing tokens.
configManagement.loadError: Unable to read platform config export.
configManagement.parseError: Import content is not valid JSON.
configManagement.empty: No platform config is available to export.

settings.title: Platform settings
settings.description: Review runtime connectivity, export or import platform configuration, and keep model, tenant policy, and runtime parameters governed from one place.
settings.governanceDescription: Review runtime connections, export governed platform data, and complete configuration migration tasks.
settings.runtimeConnectionDescription: Confirm which platform service the console is connected to and whether the current account has completed baseline setup.
settings.configScopeDescription: Exported configuration covers platform governance objects and does not include plaintext secrets.
settings.runtimeStatusDescription: Quickly verify whether model, tool, RAG, workflow, and other dependencies are connected.

migration.title: Migration checks
migration.description: Confirm object counts, import mode, and secret handling before import to avoid overwriting production configuration.
migration.notice: Configuration migration only handles platform governance data; external credentials are still managed by connectors or runtime environments.

capabilities.title: Platform rollout control
capabilities.description: Check which enterprise agent platform capabilities are wired up, then jump directly into the next setup or validation step.

model.title: Model management
model.description: Centrally manage model credentials, default models, and multi-model switching.

knowledge.title: Enterprise knowledge
knowledge.description: Upload documents, build indexes, and attach knowledge to different agent sessions.

agent.title: Agent publishing
agent.description: Publish templates as tenant instances with models, knowledge, tools, memory, and permissions.

tools.title: Enterprise tools
tools.description: Use tools to access tickets, metrics, APIs, and internal systems.

workflow.title: Automation workflow
workflow.description: Trigger agents with schedules or events to run business processes.

tenant.title: Tenant isolation
tenant.description: Isolate configuration, data, and session context by user, department, or tenant.

audit.title: Audit and permissions
audit.description: Enterprise tool calls are written to a local JSONL audit log and gated by default, tenant, and user level authorization rules.

config.title: Config migration
config.description: Export members, connectors, agents, workflows, and tool policies to copy the platform into another environment.

textInput.attachNotSupported: This model does not support file attachments

confirmCard.yesWithRule: Yes, and allow {{toolName}} to execute {{ruleContent}} for this session

chat.noMessagesDesc: Start your conversation by sending a message

agent.emptyDescription: Create an agent to get started.

session.label: Session
session.emptyHasAgent: Create a session to start chatting.

dialog-agent-create.title: Create agent
dialog-agent-create.description: Define a new agent's identity and behaviour.

identity.description: Name and instructions that define this agent's behaviour.

context-config.description: Controls how the context window is managed and compressed.

react-config.description: Controls the reasoning and action loop behaviour.

invite-config.description: Controls whether other agents can borrow this one via AgentInvite.

invite-description.label: Invite description
invite-description.placeholder: Briefly describe this agent's expertise; required when invitable is on.

dialog-session-rename.title: Rename session
dialog-session-rename.description: Give this session a new name.
dialog-session-rename.label: Name

dialog-credential-create.title: Add credential
dialog-credential-create.description: Add credentials for a model provider.

dialog-credential-edit.title: Edit Credential
dialog-credential-edit.description: Update the saved credential for this provider.

dialog-knowledge-base-create.title: Create knowledge base
dialog-knowledge-base-create.description: Create a new knowledge base using an embedding model.

descriptionField.label: Description
descriptionField.placeholder: Describe what this knowledge base is for.

policy.lockedDescription: This server already pins all knowledge bases to {{dimension}}-dim embeddings. Only compatible models are listed below.
policy.noCompatibleAny: None of your credentials expose an embedding model. Add a credential first.
policy.noCompatibleLocked: None of your credentials expose a model that can produce {{dimension}}-dim embeddings.

errors.nameRequired: Please give the knowledge base a name.

dialog-knowledge-base-edit.title: Edit knowledge base
dialog-knowledge-base-edit.description: Update the knowledge base name and description.

descriptionField.label: Description
descriptionField.placeholder: Describe what this knowledge base is for.

errors.nameRequired: Please give the knowledge base a name.

dialog-knowledge-base-delete.title: Delete knowledge base
dialog-knowledge-base-delete.description: This will permanently delete the knowledge base "{{name}}" and all of its indexed documents. This action cannot be undone.

credential.selectHintDescription: Select a credential from the sidebar to view its details.
credential.subtitle: Model API credential configuration

knowledge.subtitle: Manage knowledge bases used by your agents
knowledge.selectHintDescription: Select a knowledge base from the sidebar to view its details.

list.label: Knowledge bases
list.emptyDescription: Create a knowledge base to start indexing documents.

document.label: Documents
document.emptyDescription: Upload a document to index it into this knowledge base.
document.deleteConfirmDescription: Delete "{{name}}"? This removes the document and every indexed chunk. This action cannot be undone.
document.unsupportedExtension: "{{name}}" was skipped — file type not supported.

test.title: Test retrieval
test.emptyDescription: No chunks scored above the threshold for this query.

empty.title: No models available
empty.description: Add a credential to get started.

empty.title: No embedding models available
empty.description: Add a credential that supports embedding to get started.

permission-mode.label: Permission Mode
permission-mode.default-tooltip: Default mode: all operations require explicit permission (most secure)
permission-mode.accept_edits-tooltip: Auto-allow file reads/writes in working directories and filesystem commands; other operations follow normal rules
permission-mode.explore-tooltip: Read-only mode: allow read/grep/glob tools, deny write/edit/bash modification tools
permission-mode.bypass-tooltip: All operations automatically allowed without any permission checks (use in trusted/sandbox environments)
permission-mode.dont_ask-tooltip: Convert all ASK decisions to DENY; suitable for scheduled tasks or background execution when user is unavailable

dialog-mcp-create.title: Add MCP Servers
dialog-mcp-create.description: Add new MCP server(s) by configuration.
dialog-mcp-create.keepAliveDesc: Maintain state across interactions. Enable for browser-use or other long-running MCPs.
dialog-mcp-create.emptyMcpServers: No MCP servers found in configuration.

dialog-mcp-delete.skillDescription: Are you sure you want to remove this skill from the workspace?

dialog-skill-add.title: Add skill
dialog-skill-add.description: Enter the path to a skill directory to add it to the workspace.

createSchedule.description: Set up a recurring or one-time scheduled task for an agent.
createSchedule.descriptionPlaceholder: Describe what this schedule should do...
createSchedule.title: Create Schedule

schedule.noExecutionsDescription: This schedule has not been executed yet.
schedule.noSchedulesDescription: Create a schedule to automate agent tasks.
schedule.statefulDesc: Reuse one session across all fires to accumulate context. Disabled: every fire creates a fresh session.

model-parameters.title: Model Parameters
model-parameters.description: Adjust inference parameters for the selected model.
model-parameters.ttsParametersDescription: Adjust parameters for the selected TTS model.

setup.title: Connect to Service
setup.description: Enter your server URL and username to get started.
setup.localDescription: Use the local platform API and test user.
setup.hint: This is a frontend-only application, and all data are served from the Server URL above. The username is used solely for server-side authentication.

createAgent.title: Create an agent
createAgent.content: Start by creating an agent — your chat partner.

createSession.title: Create a session
createSession.content: Click + to start a new session.

llmSelect.title: Pick a model
llmSelect.content: Choose a model; add a credential from the dropdown if needed.

permissionMode.title: Permission mode
permissionMode.content: Decide whether tool calls need confirmation.

chatInput.title: Start chatting
chatInput.content: Type a message and press Enter. Paperclip attaches files.

plan.title: Plan
plan.emptyDescription: Tasks the agent plans will show up here.

mcp.title: MCP
mcp.description: The MCP servers equipped in the workspace.
mcp.emptyDescription: Add an MCP server to equip the workspace with tools.

skill.title: Skills
skill.description: The skills equipped in the workspace.
skill.emptyDescription: Add a skill to equip the workspace.

knowledge.title: Knowledge Bases
knowledge.description: Knowledge bases the agent can retrieve from.
knowledge.emptyDescription: Create one on the Knowledge Bases page first.

permission.title: Permissions
permission.description: The permissions applied in this session.
permission.emptyDescription: Allow / deny / ask rules will appear here as they are added.

team-sidebar.noMembers: No members yet — ask the leader to spawn one with AgentCreate.
